rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RAYMOND JAIR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Approving Assignment Of Interest In Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues to be cleared or supplemented: (1) Need clarification of the petitioner's standing/capacity to bring this petition. trustee, attorney-in-fact, counsel of Jack Jair or others. Petition should be signed and verified if the petition is brought in a fiduciary capacity. (2) Need an explanation of why the court's approval is necessary for Mabel to assign the shares to the Trust after receiving them from Raymond's estate. (3) Need notice of hearing to be given to the counsel for Sylvia Fong and Sharon So-Epperct. CRC 7.51(b). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ADA DEGHETTO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/23/2025. Issues with Thomas Larsen's petition necessitating a continuance: 1) The response to David Larsen's objection states that the two vacant lots in Lancaster, California, are of "dubious or insignificant value" and would take months or years to resolve and distribute. In a verified supplement to the petition, please explain why these properties have not been inventoried and appraised, and why marshalling and distributing these assets would take months or years to achieve. 2) The response asserts that the properties that the Lancaster properties should be "handled" under the omnibus clause. The petition for final distribution requests that distribution of any other property of the decedent "not now known or discovered" be distributed to the Estate of Nives Mercedes Nash. Here, the property is an asset of the estate that does seem to be known. Please provide authority for distribution of known property of the estate by way of the omnibus clause. 3) Any party lodging a proposed order in a contested matter must file proof of service of the proposed order on all parties entitled to notice of the petition (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A.4). Issues with David Larsen's objection: 1) No proof of service is attached to the objection filed February 19, 2025, or the second objection filed March 14, 2025. Please give notice to the executor and to his attorney as set forth in Probate Code Section 1215. Please provide courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 04/14/2025, to avoid further continuance.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF GRABIEL RIVERA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The accounting was submitted by the petitioner, who appears to have acted as a limited conservator of the estate. However, neither the appointment order nor the underlying petition outlined any specific powers or duties authorized to the petitioner. See Probate Code sections 1821(j)(1)(A) and 1830(b). Consequently, the petitioner seems to lack the authority, in the capacity of limited conservator of the estate, to manage or marshal the limited conservatee's inheritance from CALPERS and other finances. The Court is to determine whether to correct its appointment or on its own motion to remove the limited conservatorship of the estate as a clerical error, and if so, whether to take this accounting petition off calendar, as no court accountings are required unless and until a limited conservatorship of the estate is properly established.  If a conservator for the estate of Grabiel Rivera is deemed necessary, a new petition must be filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Return Of Property; Conversion; Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; Constructive Fraud; Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance; Double Damages; And Attorney?S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/05/2025 (no earlier date available) for the following issues to be addressed: 1) The date of service (see item 4a of proof of service attached to the DE-115 form filed on 03/10/2025) must be on or after the filing date of the amended petition (03/10/2025). See LRSF 14.14(A). 2) Each respondent against whom relief under Probate Code section 850 is asserted must be served in the manner required by CCP sections 413.10 et seq. at least 30 days before the hearing (see section 851(a)). Please note: If a matter is contested, a copy of the proposed order must be served on each person entitled to notice. Proof service must be filed as a separate document (not as an attachment to the proposed ordere merely lodged). See LRSF 14.7(A)(4).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARTIN GARY DRUCKER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/19/25. (No earlier availability.) (1) A petition for final distribution must state the date on which notice was mailed to the FTB. [LRSF 14.35 I.2.] (2) Notwithstanding a sole beneficiary executor's prayer, attorney costs must be itemized for the Court to determine if they were reasonable. Itemization must show the date, payee, description, and amount of each cost. (3) Paragraph 11 of the petition indicates that expenses of administration have not been paid. (4) The I&A shows the only asset to be a Treasury Direct account, which is ordinarily bonds, not cash. Non-cash assets must be appraised by a probate referee. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/5/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THEODORE PAUL JONES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the judicial officer to review the file and determine whether to take this petition off calendar or deny without prejudice for lack of satisfactory progress to address the following issues (last updated and e-mailed to counsel before the 01/13/2025 hearing): 1) Item 4 of the DE-121 form filed on 12/26/2024 was not checked. New notice must given. 2) Please provide facts in support of the allegation at item 7b(3) of the amended petition that the decedent's predeceased spouse had no interest in the estate real property listed at item 3d(4). 3) Re the attorney declaration filed on 12/16/2024: i) Did each uncle of the decedent have any issue? ii) Re "unknown sibling of Thomas Watson Jones", did this person predecease? iii) No family tree has been provided for the decedent's predeceased spouse. The family tree must show the predeceased spouse's relatives who would have been the decedent's heirs under Probate Code section 6402(e) or (g). 4) Did the decedent handwrite the month (May or 5) to date the will? Only the day (13) and year (20) handwritten above the decedent's signature are legible. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box.
